algorithm: { The algorithm first selects a promising solution from the archive based on the Pareto dominance of the objective values, then applies a hybrid local search operator that involves a random node swap followed by a greedy insertion to enhance the solution quality while ensuring the feasibility of the TSP tour. }
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)  # Randomly selecting for simplicity

    # Step 2: Convert to a list for manipulation
    current_tour = selected_solution.tolist()
    
    # Step 3: Randomly select two distinct nodes to swap
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(range(len(current_tour)), 2)
    current_tour[idx1], current_tour[idx2] = current_tour[idx2], current_tour[idx1]  # Swap
    
    # Step 4: Greedily insert one of the swapped nodes into a new position
    swapped_node = current_tour[idx1]  # Assume idx1 was swapped
    current_tour.remove(swapped_node)  # Remove to reinsert
    insert_position = random.randint(1, len(current_tour) - 1)  # Random position to insert
    current_tour.insert(insert_position, swapped_node)  # Insert back
    
    # Step 5: Convert back to a numpy array
    neighbor_solution = np.array(current_tour)
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive based on their potential for improvement, weighted by a combination of their costs in both objective spaces, then apply a creative local search strategy that involves randomly selecting a node and performing a series of moves that include both a random swap with an adjacent node and a directional shift of a randomly selected subsequence of nodes, ensuring the resulting tour remains valid while aiming to enhance the quality in both objective spaces.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1 / (obj[0] + obj[1]) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]

    # Convert selected solution to list for manipulation
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    
    n = len(neighbor_solution)

    # Randomly select a node to swap with its adjacent node
    pos = random.randint(0, n - 1)
    swap_pos = (pos + 1) % n  # wrap around for circular tour
    neighbor_solution[pos], neighbor_solution[swap_pos] = neighbor_solution[swap_pos], neighbor_solution[pos]

    # Randomly select a segment of the tour to reverse
    start, end = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))
    segment_to_reverse = neighbor_solution[start:end + 1][::-1]

    # Replace the segment in the neighbor_solution
    neighbor_solution[start:end + 1] = segment_to_reverse

    return neighbor_solution

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    weights = [1 / (obj[0] + 1e-6) * (1 / (obj[1] + 1e-6)) for _, obj in archive]  # Weight based on both objective costs
    selected_solution = random.choices(archive, weights=weights, k=1)[0][0]
    
    # Step 2: Generate a neighbor solution using a novel edge exchange strategy
    num_nodes = len(selected_solution)
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    
    # Randomly select two edges to perform the modification
    i, j = random.sample(range(num_nodes), 2)
    
    # Ensure the indices are properly ordered
    if i > j:
        i, j = j, i
    
    # Step 3: Select two new nodes to swap in place of the selected edges
    # Ensure the new nodes are not the same as the selected ones
    potential_nodes = set(range(num_nodes)) - {neighbor_solution[i], neighbor_solution[j]}
    new_nodes = random.sample(potential_nodes, 2)

    # Step 4: Create a neighbor solution by replacing the edges
    neighbor_solution[i] = new_nodes[0]
    neighbor_solution[j] = new_nodes[1]
    
    # Step 5: Ensuring the solution remains a valid TSP tour; reordering if necessary
    neighbor_solution = np.unique(neighbor_solution, return_index=True)[0]
    neighbor_solution = neighbor_solution[:num_nodes]  # Ensure it is the same length
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive using a weighted random choice based on the inverse of the sum of both objective values, then apply a creative local search strategy that includes randomly swapping two nodes in the tour and randomly selecting a segment of the tour to reverse its order, ensuring the resulting tour remains feasible while aiming for higher quality solutions in both objective spaces.}

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1 / (obj[0] + obj[1]) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]

    # Convert selected solution to list for manipulation
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()

    # Randomly select two positions to swap
    n = len(neighbor_solution)
    pos1, pos2 = random.sample(range(n), 2)

    # Swap the selected positions
    neighbor_solution[pos1], neighbor_solution[pos2] = neighbor_solution[pos2], neighbor_solution[pos1]

    # Randomly select a segment of the tour to reverse
    start, end = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))
    segment_to_reverse = neighbor_solution[start:end + 1][::-1]

    # Replace the segment in the neighbor_solution
    neighbor_solution[start:end + 1] = segment_to_reverse

    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {First, we randomly select a promising solution from the archive based on its objective values, prioritizing those with lower costs in both dimensions, then we apply a novel local search strategy that involves randomly selecting two non-adjacent nodes in the tour and swapping them while ensuring the feasibility of the tour is maintained, which allows for exploration of the solution space without violating TSP constraints, and finally, we return the modified tour as the neighbor solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, selected_objective = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Generate a neighbor solution
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    n = len(neighbor_solution)

    # Randomly select two non-adjacent indices to swap
    while True:
        i, j = random.sample(range(n), 2)
        if abs(i - j) > 1 and not (i == 0 and j == n - 1) and not (j == 0 and i == n - 1):  # Ensure non-adjacency
            break

    # Swap the two selected nodes
    neighbor_solution[i], neighbor_solution[j] = neighbor_solution[j], neighbor_solution[i]

    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive based on the promise of improvement by using an intelligent random selection weighted by the inverse of their objective values, then apply a creative local search strategy which involves a hybrid of random node swapping and a directional shift where we randomly select a sequence of consecutive nodes to reverse their order, ensuring that the resulting tour remains valid and feasible while potentially yielding a higher quality solution in both objective spaces.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1 / (obj[0] + obj[1]) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]

    # Convert selected solution to list for manipulation
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()

    # Randomly select two positions to swap
    n = len(neighbor_solution)
    pos1, pos2 = random.sample(range(n), 2)

    # Swap the selected positions
    neighbor_solution[pos1], neighbor_solution[pos2] = neighbor_solution[pos2], neighbor_solution[pos1]

    # Randomly select a segment of the tour to reverse
    start, end = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))
    segment_to_reverse = neighbor_solution[start:end + 1][::-1]

    # Replace the segment in the neighbor_solution
    neighbor_solution[start:end + 1] = segment_to_reverse

    return neighbor_solution
